title: Royal Caribbean (RCL) Up 34% in 6 Months: More Room to Run?

title: ROYAL CARIBBEAN'S ICON OF THE SEAS UPS THE ANTE AND APPETITE WITH NEW, REIMAGINED DINING
text: More than 20 Choices for Every Occasion Include a New Eight-Course Experience and the Cruise Line's First Food Hall   MIAMI , May 25, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Royal Caribbean International's Icon of the Seas is turning the tables in January 2024 with an unmatched lineup of dining experiences designed for every type of family and vacationer. Travelers with an appetite for adventure can have their pick of more than 20 new and signature ways to dine and make memories, a combination of elevated experiences, spots for quick bites and venues made for families with kids.

title: 3 Reasons Royal Caribbean Stock Can Keep Floating Higher
text: The onset of the coronavirus pandemic sunk the stock to a 10-year low under $20. Optimism about a recovery brought the popular reopening play back to nearly $100.

title: Royal Caribbean (RCL) Stock Up on Q1 Earnings & Revenue Beat
text: Royal Caribbean's (RCL) first-quarter 2023 results benefit from strong cruising demand, better pricing on close-in demand and strong onboard spending.

title: Royal Caribbean posts better than expected 1Q loss and boosts guidance; shares propel higher
text: Royal Caribbean Group shares climbed 7% to $71.72 in late Thursday morning trading after the cruise ship operator announced that its first-quarter 2023 net loss narrowed to $47.9 million, or $0.19 a share, from $1.17 billion, or $4.58 per share, during the same period last year. The company's 1Q adjusted per-share loss of $0.23 surpassed the FactSet compiled consensus estimate of a $0.69 per-share deficit.

title: Royal Caribbean Stock Surges. Consumers Are Still Spending on Cruises.
text: The company said demand was above historical rates as it reported a narrower quarterly loss than expected and raised its financial forecasts.

title: Royal Caribbean stock rallies after much narrower-than-expected loss and raised profit outlook, as demand has ‘swiftly' accelerated
text: Shares of Royal Caribbean Group RCL, -1.08% ran up 7.4% toward a two-month high in premarket trading Thursday, after the cruise operator reported a much narrower-than-expected loss and raised its full-year profit outlook, as demand “swiftly” accelerated above historical trends. The net loss narrowed to $47.9 million, or 19 cents a share, from $1.17 billion, or $4.58 a share, in the year-ago period.

title: ROYAL CARIBBEAN GROUP REPORTS FIRST QUARTER EARNINGS AND INCREASES FULL YEAR GUIDANCE ON STRONG REVENUE OUTLOOK
text: MIAMI, May 4, 2023 /PRNewswire/ — Royal Caribbean Group (NYSE: RCL) today reported first quarter Loss per Share of $(0.19) and Adjusted Loss per Share of $(0.23). These results were significantly better than the company’s guidance primarily due to strong close-in bookings at higher prices, continued strength of onboard spend, and favorable timing of operating costs. title: ROYAL CARIBBEAN GROUP REPORTS FIRST QUARTER EARNINGS AND INCREASES FULL YEAR GUIDANCE ON STRONG REVENUE OUTLOOK
text: MIAMI , May 4, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Royal Caribbean Group (NYSE: RCL) today reported first quarter Loss per Share of $(0.19) and Adjusted Loss per Share of $(0.23). These results were significantly better than the company's guidance primarily due to strong close-in bookings at higher prices, continued strength of onboard spend, and favorable timing of operating costs.

title: Royal Caribbean (RCL) to Report Q1 Earnings: What to Expect
text: Royal Caribbean's (RCL) first-quarter 2023 results are likely to benefit from solid cruising demand and onboard spending and better pricing on close-in demand. Yet, high costs might have hurt its performance.
